target.name: Lightelligence
target.founder: Shen Yichen, Marin Soljačić, Huaiyu Meng, Spencer Powers

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Lightelligence is a public-stage semiconductor company that has raised $270M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of three same-sector, same-stage peers: Credo Semiconductor, Ouster, and Aeva Technologies, alongside a broader backdrop of eight private frontier-chip companies and three public semiconductor benchmarks.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Across the three direct comparables, valuations range from $1.0B (Aeva Technologies) to $5.0B (Credo Semiconductor), with a median of $1.5B. This method is well-suited given Lightelligence's stage and sector alignment with the peer set. A public-comp anchoring approach using price-to-sales multiples from public semiconductor names (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) at a median 24.2x was not applied, as Lightelligence's revenue is not disclosed—standard for private companies and prerequisite for multiple-based valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on a $1.5B median. This range reflects the peer-median result with modest sensitivity bounds. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and profitability trajectory relative to the comparables, neither of which is provided. The single-method foundation also limits confidence; revenue disclosure or additional operating metrics would enable cross-validation via revenue multiple approaches and strengthen the valuation case.
